basic_details.job_title: IRCTC Hospitality Monitor Recruitment 2026 Notification Out – Walk in for 49 Posts
basic_details.organization_name: Indian Railway Catering and Tourism Corporation Ltd. (IRCTC), East Zone
basic_details.total_vacancies: 49 Posts (Reservation as per Govt. of India policy)
basic_details.short_information: The latest Indian Railway Catering and Tourism Corporation Ltd. (IRCTC), East Zone Recruitment 2026 has been announced for Hospitality Monitor (Contractual) posts! Published on 27.04.2026 at IRCTC Official Website, this new recruitment drive offers 49 openings for eligible candidates. Applicants within the upper age limit of 27 years (with age relaxations for reserved categories as per Govt. rules) and possessing a relevant B.Sc / BBA / MBA qualification in Hospitality, Hotel Management, Culinary Arts, or Tourism Management can attend the walk-in interviews from 18-05-2026 to 20-05-2026. Find

age_limit.age_details: Contractual – 2 Years (Extendable by 1 year based on requirement and satisfactory performance)
age_limit.details: The upper age limit is calculated as on the cut-off date 27-04-2026. Age relaxation is applicable as per Govt. of India rules for reserved category candidates.

qualification.details: Only Indian Nationals are eligible to apply. The cut-off date for age, qualification, experience and other purposes is 27/04/2026. Engagement is subject to medical fitness test as prescribed by IRCTC. Selected candidates must furnish a security deposit of Rs. 25,000/- in the form of a DD payable at Kolkata. Knowledge of computer (MS Office) and preparation of reports is desirable. Ability to solve problems and make rational decisions is required. | Educational Qualification

vacancy_details.No. of Posts: 49 (Reservation as per Govt. of India policy)
vacancy_details.Consolidated Remuneration: Rs. 30,000/- p.m. + DA Rs. 350/- per day onboard + Lodging Rs. 240/- per night stay + NHA Rs. 384/- per NH worked + Medical Insurance (as per IRCTC guidelines)

category_wise_vacancy.No. of Posts: 49 (Reservation as per Govt. of India policy)

selection_process: Stage 1 Download and fill Application Form (proforma attached to notification) complete in all respects
Stage 2 Walk-in Interview at IRCTC Zonal Office, Kolkata on 18, 19 or 20 May 2026 (10:30 AM to 04:30 PM) with Application Form, original documents and one set of attested photocopies
Stage 3 Personal Interview based on credentials; selection in order of merit
Stage 4 Verification of antecedents and medical fitness test as prescribed by IRCTC
Stage 5 Offer of engagement issued to suitable candidates; selected candidates to furnish security deposit of Rs. 25,000/- (DD payable at Kolkata)

The selection process for IRCTC East Zone Hospitality Monitor Recruitment 2026 is through Walk-in Interview only. Interview will be conducted based on the credentials of the candidates. Based on performance in the personal interview

candidates will be selected and the offer of engagement shall be issued to suitable candidates in the order of merit and number of vacancies. Final engagement is subject to verification of antecedents and medical fitness test as prescribed by IRCTC.

This engagement is purely contractual in nature and will not entitle any candidate to claim for regular/permanent employment in IRCTC. The contract may be terminated by giving one month's notice by either side; if performance is not found satisfactory
the contract may be terminated with 15 days' notice.
how_to_apply: Eligible and qualified candidates must attend the walk-in interview on any of the three dates: 18th, 19th or 20th May 2026 between 10:30 AM and 04:30 PM at IRCTC Zonal Office, 3, Koilaghat Street, Ground Floor, Kolkata – 700 001. There is no prior online application on the IRCTC website. Candidates must fill the Application Form (proforma attached to the notification) complete in all respects and submit it at the venue of the interview.

Candidates must bring the complete Application Form along with original documents, one set of attested copies of required documents (caste, education & experience), and three recent passport size photographs. In the absence of any required original document, the candidate will not be allowed to appear in the walk-in interview.

Those working in Govt./PSUs may apply through proper channel/submit NOC at the time of interview or submit a proper relieving letter from the present employer in the event of selection in IRCTC. In case of extension of interview, candidates will have to arrange their own stay and food. No TA/DA will be paid for attending the walk-in interview.

Any corrigendum/clarifications to this notification (including change of date and place of interview), if necessary, will be uploaded on IRCTC website at www.irctc.com under HR&Career > Recruitment > New Opening only; no separate press coverage will be given in newspapers.
Documents Required at the Time of Walk-in Interview (Original + One Set of Self-Attested Photocopy):

Graduation year-wise All Marksheets & Certificate/Degree (Provisional Certificate/Degree from concerned University also acceptable). Candidates passed from IHMs must carry all six semesters marksheets, consolidated marksheet from IGNOU and degree/certificate from IGNOU.
Post-Graduation or any other higher academic/education marksheets & certificate (year-wise) – if applicable
All Experience Certificates in chronological order
Caste Certificate for SC/ST/OBC category (OBC certificate must be for Non-Creamy Layer and not more than 1 year old)
Duly filled Application Form (proforma as per notification)
important_instructions: Only Indian Nationals are eligible to apply. This engagement is purely contractual and does not entitle any candidate to claim regular/permanent employment in IRCTC. The cut-off date for age, qualification, experience and other purposes is 27-04-2026. Engagement is subject to medical fitness test as prescribed by IRCTC. Selected candidates must furnish a security deposit of Rs. 25,000/- in the form of a DD payable at Kolkata. The contract period is 2 years extendable by 1 year; contract may be terminated with one month's notice by either side; with 15 days' notice if performance is unsatisfactory. Candidates working in Govt./PSUs must apply through proper channel/submit NOC at interview or submit a proper relieving letter from the present employer on selection. In the absence of any required original document at the time of walk-in interview, the candidate will not be allowed to appear in the interview. OBC certificate must be for Non-Creamy Layer category and should not be more than 1 year old. Knowledge of computer (MS Office) and preparation of reports is desirable. Submission of false/incorrect information or suppression of any relevant facts at any stage will result in summary termination of candidature/services. No TA/DA will be paid to candidates for attending the walk-in interview. In case of extension of interview, candidates must arrange their own stay and food. Selected candidates may be posted in North East States/West Bengal/Bihar & Jharkhand; however, they may be transferred/posted anywhere in India at IRCTC's discretion. IRCTC reserves the right to cancel/amend the advertisement and/or the selection process thereunder. Any corrigendum/clarifications will be uploaded only on www.irctc.com under HR&Career > Recruitment > New Opening; no separate press coverage will be given.

faq.question: Q1. How many vacancies are there in IRCTC East Zone Hospitality Monitor Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Indian Railway Catering and Tourism Corporation Ltd. (IRCTC), East Zone has notified 49 Hospitality Monitor vacancies on a contractual basis under Notification No. IRCTC/EZ/HRD/Recruit./Contract./HM-VI/2026/01.

faq.question: Q2. What is the salary for IRCTC East Zone Hospitality Monitor 2026?
faq.answer: Selected Hospitality Monitors will receive Rs. 30,000 per month as consolidated remuneration, plus daily allowance of Rs. 350, lodging charges of Rs. 240 per night, National Holiday Allowance of Rs. 384 per NH worked, and medical insurance reimbursement as per IRCTC guidelines.

faq.question: Q3. What are the walk-in interview dates for IRCTC East Zone Hospitality Monitor Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The walk‑in interviews for IRCTC East Zone Hospitality Monitor 2026 will be held on 18 May 2026, 19 May 2026, and 20 May 2026 between 10:30 AM and 04:30 PM.

faq.question: Q4. Where is the IRCTC East Zone Hospitality Monitor walk-in venue?
faq.answer: Candidates must appear for the walk‑in interview at IRCTC Zonal Office, 3, Koilaghat Street, Ground Floor, Kolkata – 700 001 on any of the scheduled dates.

faq.question: Q5. What is the eligibility qualification for IRCTC Hospitality Monitor Recruitment 2026 (East Zone)?
faq.answer: Applicants must possess a full‑time B.Sc in Hospitality & Hotel Administration, BBA/MBA (Culinary Arts), B.Sc Hotel Management & Catering Science, or MBA (Tourism and Hotel Management) from institutes/universities recognized by NCHMCT/UGC/AICTE/Govt. of India/State Govt.

faq.question: Q6. What is the age limit for IRCTC East Zone Hospitality Monitor 2026?
faq.answer: The upper age limit is 27 years as on 27 April 2026, with relaxations as per Government of India rules for OBC, SC, ST and PwBD candidates.

faq.question: Q7. Is prior experience mandatory for IRCTC East Zone Hospitality Monitor posts?
faq.answer: No, freshers with the required hospitality or tourism qualification are eligible, as no prior work experience is mandatory for the Hospitality Monitor post.

faq.question: Q8. What is the nature and tenure of IRCTC East Zone Hospitality Monitor engagement?
faq.answer: The Hospitality Monitor engagement is purely contractual for 2 years, extendable by 1 additional year based on IRCTC’s requirement and the candidate’s satisfactory performance.

faq.question: Q9. Is there any security deposit for selected IRCTC Hospitality Monitor candidates?
faq.answer: Yes, provisionally selected candidates must deposit Rs. 25,000 as a security deposit in the form of a Demand Draft payable at Kolkata before joining.

faq.question: Q10. Does IRCTC Hospitality Monitor Recruitment 2026 provide permanent government job?
faq.answer: No, this recruitment is purely contractual; it does not confer any right to regular or permanent employment in IRCTC or Indian Railways even after successful completion of the contract period.
